body:
## Summary
- publish Sepo preview URLs through GitHub Deployment statuses instead of PR comments
- use the resolved Sepo auth token when it can create deployments, with GITHUB_TOKEN fallback
- mark matching preview deployments inactive when the PR closes
- update README preview docs

## Validation
- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(".github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml"); puts "yaml ok"'\n- git diff --check

comments.body: [vc]: #D3ydA0WTO2xH8yj6wUQnsrJ8q5pACZAlkxcS857G9Ow=: The latest updates on your projects. Learn more about [Vercel for GitHub](https://vercel.link/github-learn-more). | Project | Deployment | Actions | Updated (UTC) | | :--- | :----- | :------ | :------ | | [literature-template](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template) | ![Ready](https://vercel.com/static/status/ready.svg) [Ready](https://vercel.com/codiscover-ai/literature-template/HzRHawGNyuzCtbV1HzovjHJ7JTde) | [Preview](https://literature-template-git-agent-preview-depl-3420fd-codiscover-ai.vercel.app), [Comment](https://vercel.live/open-feedback/literature-template-git-agent-preview-depl-3420fd-codiscover-ai.vercel.app?via=pr-comment-feedback-link) | Jun 5, 2026 4:25pm |
@sepo-agent /answer should we consider all the implementation in `.agent` with the corresponding cli server?

Yes, but not **all** of it.

I’d move the reusable GitHub Deployment/status logic into `.agent` behind a CLI, because PR #22 currently embeds fairly complex deployment creation, token fallback, and inactive-marking shell directly in the workflow: `.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml` lines [64-154](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/agent/preview-deployment-status-events/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L64-L154) and [179-213](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/agent/preview-deployment-status-events/.github/workflows/agent-site-preview.yml#L179-L213). That matches the existing `.agent` pattern where deterministic GitHub operations are wrapped in CLI entrypoints, e.g. comments in [`post-comment.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/.agent/src/cli/post-comment.ts) and shared `gh` helpers in [`github.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/.agent/src/github.ts#L1-L4).

I would **not** move the site build, artifact upload, or preview-server OIDC deploy/teardown call just for purity. Those are workflow/server integration steps, and README already describes them as the workflow asking `preview-api.sepo.sh` to publish the artifact: [README.md#L209](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/agent/preview-deployment-status-events/README.md#L209).

Suggested plan:

1. Add a `.agent/src/cli/publish-preview-deployment.ts` CLI for creating the deployment and success status.
2. Add a `.agent/src/cli/inactivate-preview-deployments.ts` CLI for finding Sepo preview deployments by PR/SHA and marking them inactive.
3. Put payload construction and `gh api` calls in reusable `.agent/src/github.ts` helpers.
4. Replace the large inline workflow shell blocks with short `node .agent/dist/cli/...` calls.
5. Add focused CLI tests for token fallback, payload shape, matching by payload PR/SHA, and no-match teardown behavior.
6. Keep the preview server `curl` deploy/teardown calls in the workflow unless we decide to make preview-server integration reusable across multiple workflows.

So: yes, we should consider `.agent` CLI coverage for the deterministic GitHub-side implementation, but keep workflow-only and preview-server plumbing outside unless reuse pressure appears.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

title: Use deployment events for Sepo previews
